quick_skim.title: Projected McKean--Vlasov Dynamics for Entropic Weak Optimal Transport
quick_skim.abstract: Unlike classical optimal transport, weak transport costs depend nonlinearly on the conditional law of couplings. This feature is essential in problems involving barycenter, conditional moments, and martingale-type constraints. Meanwhile, such conditional dependence makes ordinary Wasserstein geometry insufficient and calls instead for an adapted Wasserstein viewpoint. In this paper, we investigate the entropy-regularized weak optimal transport via gradient flows in adapted Wasserstein space.   We derive, from the formal tangent structure of adapted Wasserstein space and the projection onto the set of couplings with prescribed marginals, a coupled McKean--Vlasov SDE. A novel and subtle term is a projection that, at each $Y$-location, averages a weak-transport force that already depends on the conditional law of $Y$ given $X$, thereby preserving marginals while retaining the nonlinear weak-transport structure.   Under mild integrability and regularity assumptions, we prove weak existence and uniqueness in law for this projected McKean--Vlasov equation. We then prove that the flow converges, in the adapted Wasserstein topology, to the unique minimizer of the entropic weak optimal transport problem. We also describe a particle approximation and illustrate the dynamics on optimal transport and martingale optimal transport examples.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: entropic optimal transport connects to Schrodinger bridge

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Investigates entropic weak optimal transport via gradient flows in adapted Wasserstein space, deriving a coupled McKean-Vlasov SDE with projection, providing a method relevant to constructing diffusion bridges.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文研究熵正则化弱最优传输问题，在适应Wasserstein空间中通过梯度流推导出耦合的McKean-Vlasov随机微分方程，其中包含一个新颖的投影项。该方法为构建扩散桥提供了理论工具，特别与薛定谔桥问题紧密相关。

quick_skim.title: Riemannian Diffusion Models on General Manifolds via Physics-Informed Neural Networks
quick_skim.abstract: Riemannian diffusion models generalize score-based generative modeling to manifold-supported data via stochastic diffusion equations on the manifold. However, training requires sampling from and differentiating the manifold heat kernel, which is rarely available in closed form beyond a few highly symmetric manifolds. We propose a general approach that approximates the heat kernel by directly solving the manifold heat equation with a physics-informed neural network (PINN). Given an explicit manifold specification, we choose a coordinate system, derive the corresponding heat (Fokker--Planck) equation and a short-time asymptotic approximation, and then train a PINN to learn the log heat kernel. The resulting surrogate enables both forward noising (heat-kernel sampling) and conditional-score evaluation for denoising score matching. We demonstrate the method on diverse manifolds including $S^2$, $SO(3)$, $\mathrm{SPD}(n)$, and permutation-quotiented point clouds.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: heat kernel approximation useful for diffusion bridges on manifolds
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 热核近似可用于流形上的扩散桥

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a PINN to approximate the heat kernel on manifolds, enabling score-based generative modeling and potentially supporting diffusion bridge construction on arbitrary manifolds.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对黎曼流形上的扩散模型，提出使用物理信息神经网络（PINN）直接求解流形热方程来近似热核，避免了闭式热核的局限性。该方法为流形上的扩散生成建模提供了通用工具，也可用于构建流形上的扩散桥。

quick_skim.title: Generating quantum ensembles via reverse-time quantum diffusions
quick_skim.abstract: We establish a reverse-time denoising theory for quantum diffusions of continuously measured quantum systems. Starting from the stochastic Schrödinger equation of a forward noising dynamics, we derive the exact reverse-time dynamics for quantum trajectories, whose law coincides with the time-reversal of the original process. We prove that the denoising dynamics is a physically admissible quantum diffusion, with the same measurement-induced noise but a state-dependent feedback Hamiltonian, a direct analogue of the "score function" of generative classical diffusion models. This provides a principled framework for converting samples of a simple distribution into those of a more complex ensemble of quantum states. We show how the denoising dynamics can be directly learnt from forward trajectory data, and how to exploit purification to initialise the denoising process.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: reverse-time quantum diffusions generate target ensemble
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 反向时间量子扩散生成目标系综
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 反向时间量子扩散生成目标系综

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Establishes reverse-time denoising for quantum diffusions, analogous to score-based diffusion models, enabling generation of complex quantum ensembles from simple distributions.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对连续测量量子系统的量子扩散，建立了精确的反向时间去噪理论，其去噪动力学类似经典扩散模型的分数函数。该框架将简单分布的样本转换为复杂量子态系综，实现了量子版本的扩散桥。通过类比反向扩散过程，为量子态生成提供了可解释的机制。
